provider.name: Ryan Fraley
provider.address: 2015 W Broadway St, Unit 3A, Ardmore, OK 73401

context: Month 17 of 24 post-Hansen bilateral rib reconstruction (Day ~515)
Critical collagen remodeling window (months 12-18) — we're IN IT
Extended recovery timeline discovered: 29-41 months total (Hansen Sep 2025 phone consult)
Bioabsorbable PLLA/PLGA plates degrading — biological fusion still forming
Independent clinical review: A- grade (ChatGPT GPT-5.4 analysis)
Left-chain compensation cascade validated: labral tear → rib instability → self-reorg → thumb MCP

concerns.title: Breathing dysfunction
concerns.detail: Persistent thoracic extension compensation during breathing — diaphragm pulls down but unsecured ribs trigger elevation through low back extension. Foundational issue affecting all exercises.

concerns.title: Scapulothoracic interface dysfunction
concerns.detail: Left scapula elevated, 'bunch of fluff' tissue posterior preventing proper seating. Position-dependent clicking eliminated by forward lean.

concerns.title: Pectoral engagement severely limited (~2-3/10)
concerns.detail: Key recovery indicator; muscles guarding/spasming to protect unstable ribs. Compensatory patterns from 17 months post-surgery.

concerns.title: Left-chain compensation cascade
concerns.detail: Labral tear → rib instability → manual self-reorg → thumb MCP overload. Elite compensations from yoga teacher training deeply baked in.

sinceLastSession: Reviewed Dr. Hansen's surgical notes with Claude — extracted spacer graft details, confirmed rib 7 NOT involved
Discovered extended recovery timeline (29-41 months vs previously thought ~24)
Continued mirror-based diaphragmatic breathing work (shirt off)
Ongoing self-repositioning behavior — pushing ribs forward, bracing at coracoid/clavicle area
findings: Left scapular clicking during arm bike is strongly position-dependent — eliminated entirely by forward lean. Most reproducible symptom modifier in 8 sessions.
Left scapula remains elevated compared to right — persistent SICK scapula presentation
'Bunch of fluff' tissue posterior to scapula preventing scapulothoracic contact — fibrotic adhesion or altered muscle tone
Rib cage hypothesis: ribs backing into scapular space — pushing ribs forward manually gives shoulder blade space
AC joint involvement hypothesized (pec minor → coracoid → AC shear from self-repositioning) — NOT tested, no provocation tests performed
Ryan genuinely confused by Hansen's 3-year recovery timeline — volunteered to call Dr. Hansen
Breath-holding during exercises caught — mental engagement displacing respiratory awareness
Rotational hip exercise produced immediate postural improvement ('half inch taller' sensation)
prescribed: Rotational hip exercise with wall contact — push hip to front door, coordinate head positioning
Core crunches with scapular initiation — start between shoulder blades, pull hands toward knees

morning: 15g collagen + 500mg Vitamin C + 20oz water before moving
Diaphragmatic reset: 10 min posterior rib breathing (crocodile position, 4-4-4 pattern)
Log roll exit from bed (no trunk rotation)
Peroneal release (R foot) — tennis ball under lateral foot, 2-3 min
Hip extension stretch — standing, 30sec x3 (releases psoas, stops anterior rib flare)
Pectoral engagement check (daily 0-10 metric)
protocol.supplements: Collagen 15g AM, Vitamin C 1000mg/day (500 x2), Magnesium 400mg PM, 80oz+ water
protocol.restrictions: No spinal rotation >45 degrees, no overhead reaching, no heavy lifting

exercises.name: Rotational hip exercise with wall contact
exercises.detail: Push hip toward front door while rotating, coordinate with head positioning, heel against back wall

exercises.name: Core crunches with scapular initiation
exercises.detail: Start crunch between shoulder blades, pull hands toward knees — initiate from upper back not abdomen

exercises.name: Outside pinky wall push
exercises.detail: Push outside pinky finger up wall surface maintaining shoulder positioning — activates posterior chain

exercises.name: Mirror-based breathing observation
exercises.detail: Diaphragmatic breathing shirtless — visualize rib cage movement, expand ribs while keeping them down, prevent thoracic extension

exercises.name: Dead hangs with scapular focus
exercises.detail: Maintain shoulder blade engagement, avoid rib cage flaring, progress from supported to full hang

exercises.name: Wall-based rotational band work
exercises.detail: Orange band, inside arm stabilized against wall, outside arm rotating against resistance

exercises.name: Cross-body diagonal band pulls
exercises.detail: Discus/spiral pattern, mid-to-high level (NOT low), spiral fascial line activation

exercises.name: Backward pulling movements
exercises.detail: Posterior chain activation, maintain form

exercises.name: Balloon breathing
exercises.detail: Inflate using belly expansion NOT chest rise, eliminate lip pursing, complete exhale with abdominal contraction — 'quick exit' sound

exercises.name: Rotational marching
exercises.detail: Alternating leg marches maintaining abdominal tension, coordinate with breathing, prevent thoracic extension

exercises.name: Facing band same side march
exercises.detail: Band high and behind, march with straight arm extension — maintain fascial tension throughout ROM

exercises.name: Thread the needle
exercises.detail: To be demonstrated by Ryan

exercises.name: Spiral/rotational fascial work
exercises.detail: 'Reverse lawnmower' movement, mid-to-high level NOT low

exercises.name: Anterior chain fly + posterior control
exercises.detail: Dual-component, front and back simultaneously

exercises.name: Pull-apart exercises
exercises.detail: Maintain form and control

exercises.name: Anti-machine lengthening + posterior rotation
exercises.detail: Session 3 prescription

exercises.name: Serratus activation
exercises.detail: Prone position, wrists ahead of elbows

exercises.name: Core/rotation work
exercises.detail: Squeeze ball, contract core, rotate toward foot — ab compression NOT shoulder extension

exercises.name: Scap strengthening
exercises.detail: Dead hang position, maintain blade engagement — progress to alternating marches

exercises.name: Scap-ups
exercises.detail: Continue, avoid compensation patterns

exercises.name: Anti-rotation band work
exercises.detail: Modified walk-outs with super/thera band — isometric eccentric control, NOT active twisting

exercises.name: Transverse abdominis activation
exercises.detail: Supine, knees bent, draw belly button to spine, hold

questions: Ryan to call Dr. Hansen — autologous approach + 3-year timeline rationale
AC joint formal testing needed — cross-body adduction, O'Brien's, Paxinos, bilateral palpation
Should self-repositioning behavior be stopped? (potential AC shear)
Amy Thompson IASTM timing — coordinate with 8-session reassessment
Lower abdominal concern — snap, sneezing pain, palpable lump at inferior rectus attachment
Thread the needle — 4th deferral, priority for Session 9
Resistance bands — 5th deferral, non-negotiable for Session 9
decompressionReset: Do NOT push rib back. Lie on R side → reach L arm overhead → deep breath into L flank → let gravity + breath reset → hold 2-3 min.

symptoms.postural_response: half inch taller from hip rotation

assignments.exercise: Rotational hip exercise with wall contact

notes: 8-session milestone. Ryan volunteered to call Dr. Hansen about 3-year recovery timeline. AC joint hypothesis needs formal testing. ~30-40% of session consumed by non-clinical conversation.

Hip extension stretch — standing, 30sec x3 (releases psoas, stops anterior rib flare)
Pectoral engagement check (daily 0-10 metric)
